title: T-105 — document Vercel-only deploy assumption for x-vercel-cron OR require Bearer-AND-header in prod (FT-045)
prompt:
# T-105 — document Vercel-only deploy assumption for x-vercel-cron OR require Bearer-AND-header in prod (FT-045)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-045** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (XS effort)

## Goal
Either a runbook locks the Vercel-only assumption or the route enforces Bearer-AND-header in `NODE_ENV=production`.

## Implementation contract
1. Add `docs/runbooks/cron-auth.md` documenting the assumption + a one-liner config for non-Vercel deploys.
2. Route enforces both Bearer AND x-vercel-cron in production.
3. Dev mode: either-or.

## Files (scope)
- docs/runbooks/cron-auth.md [create]
- apps/web/app/api/cron/[name]/route.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the cron route in production, when called with only x-vercel-cron and no Bearer, then 401.
2. Given the runbook, when followed for a non-Vercel target, then ops know how to switch to Bearer-only.
3. Given dev mode, when called with either auth mechanism, then 200.

## Test strategy
- RED: extend the existing cron-auth test pack with the production-strict branch before flipping NODE_ENV gates.
- Run `pnpm test cron-auth`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not trust x-vercel-cron in production without the platform guarantee.
- Do not skip the Bearer check in dev when set.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Document the Vercel-only deploy assumption that x-vercel-cron header can be trusted, OR enforce Bearer-AND-header in production.
pipeline_inputs.details: T-034 trusts `x-vercel-cron`. On Vercel the header is injected by the platform and cannot be spoofed; off-Vercel the assumption breaks. Either lock the deploy target to Vercel (documented) or require Bearer-AND-header in production.
out_of_scope: Do not change CRON_SECRET rotation policy.
acceptance_criteria: Given the cron route in production, when called with only x-vercel-cron and no Bearer, then 401.
Given the runbook, when followed for a non-Vercel target, then ops know how to switch to Bearer-only.
Given dev mode, when called with either auth mechanism, then 200.

Do not skip the Bearer check in dev when set.
